steps.title: Determine if the name is essential
steps.content: "Wildlife biologist Dr. Purnima Devi Barman" — the name is essential (restrictive) because the sentence is identifying which biologist. There are many wildlife biologists; the name specifies which one. Essential appositives take no commas.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
No commas around the name → biologist Dr. Purnima Devi Barman with no punctuation. A and B introduce comma(s) that wrongly mark the name as nonessential.

C inserts a colon, which doesn't fit (the preceding part isn't an independent clause that introduces).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
